Liga da Justiça.description: Brazilian militia faction based in Rio de Janeiro, founded in 1995 by Ricardo Teixeira Cruz (Batman) and Aldemar Almeida dos Santos (Robin). It is the largest and most well-known militia group in Rio, involved in assassinations, extortion, and terrorist activities. The group has been led by Toni Ângelo de Souza Aguiar (Robin) after Batman's imprisonment.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivais: A.D.A.

edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: TCP

Hook Gang.description: The Hook Gang was a street gang and river pirate group active in New York City in the 1860s and 1870s. Based in Corlear's Hook on the East River, the gang engaged in armed robbery, theft, hijacking, and river piracy. It was formed around 1866 and disbanded after police arrests in 1876.

Atlantic Guards.description: The Atlantic Guards were a 19th-century American street gang active in New York City from the 1840s to the 1860s. They were one of the original Bowery gangs, known for nativist vigilante activities and street fighting against Irish American gangs like the Dead Rabbits. They wore a red stripe on their trousers as a uniform and were allied with the Bowery Boys. The gang participated in the Dead Rabbits Riot of 1857.

Cesarano clan.description: The Cesarano clan is a Camorra clan based in Castellammare di Stabia, Italy, founded in the 1980s by Ferdinando Cesarano. It operates in the Ponte Persica region and Pompei, engaging in extortion, racketeering, usury, and drug trafficking. The clan has been led by Nicola Esposito and currently by Luigi Di Martino. Ferdinando Cesarano was sentenced to life imprisonment in 2000.

Kansas City crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Kansas City, Missouri, founded around 1912 by Sicilian mafiosi Joseph and Peter DiGiovanni. It expanded through bootlegging during Prohibition and later engaged in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, and drug trafficking. The family was led for decades by Nicholas Civella, who forged alliances with other families and used Teamsters to fund Las Vegas casinos. As of 2015, it had an estimated 12 made members and remains active.

'Ndrina Pititto.description: The 'Ndrina Pititto is a 'Ndrangheta clan based in Mileto, Vibo Valentia, Italy. Their illegal activities include extortion and drug trafficking. They were involved in the Stammer operation, which seized 8 tons of cocaine in 2017. The clan has been active since at least the 1980s.

descriptions: The Pititto-Prostamo-Iannello were in a feud with the Galati clan during the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Anni '90 - Faida con i Galati

Garduña.description: The Garduña is a mythical secret criminal society said to have been founded in Spain in the late Middle Ages, around 1417, with statutes approved in Toledo in 1420. It was allegedly involved in robbery, kidnapping, arson, and murder-for-hire. However, historians consider it a fictional organization, with all references traced back to a 19th-century book. It is often cited as a foundational myth for Italian mafias like the Camorra, Mafia, and 'Ndrangheta.

Norteños.description: The Norteños are a Latino street gang primarily operating in Northern California, affiliated with the Bloods. They originated in 1968 in California prisons as a rival faction to the Sureños, aligning with Nuestra Familia. The gang is involved in drug trafficking and violent conflicts, and has been targeted by federal RICO investigations such as Operation Black Widow.

descriptions: MOD splintered from Peace MOD.

The splinter group formed the "Masters of Destruction" (MOD).

descriptions: The two clubs engaged in a shootout at a gas station in Argentina.
